commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1f50e293d444bde80f473028b48c5737fb7bf990)
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes provider auth checks and visible model catalog resolution asynchronous, yielding before slow provider auth discovery and updating `/models`, gateway, and model-picker call sites.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was performed in this read-only review. Source inspection confirms the current auth-filter path is synchronous, and the related prewarm PR documents live timing proof, but this PR still lacks after-fix Discord slow-path proof.
commands.review_followup: Human handling is needed because the PR is protected/stacked, lacks contributor real behavior proof, and has incomplete async migration findings.

target.title: perf(models): make provider auth checks non-blocking
target.body:
## Summary

Make the provider auth check non-blocking so a `/models` cold sweep stops pinning the event loop.

When the prepared map misses (startup window, scope override), `buildModelsProviderData` falls back to per-provider compute over ~30–40 providers, each doing sync fs reads + external-CLI probes (~500 ms each). The whole sweep ran on the event loop thread, blocking Discord's 3 s ack heartbeat. Liveness diagnostics showed `eventLoopDelayP99Ms=24461.2` during a slow `/models`.

This PR turns `hasAuthForModelProvider` into a real async function with a `setImmediate` yield before the slow path. A sweep over N providers now releases the event loop between providers, so Discord acks land on time while the catalog-auth filter runs.

Total sync cpu time is unchanged — the leaves (`fs.readFileSync`, `execSync` in `auth-profiles/store.ts`) are still sync. That conversion is a much bigger refactor and is left for a follow-up; the yield point alone gives the event-loop responsiveness win.

## Stack

This PR is stacked on top of [#???](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/new/sjf/discord-picker-pagination) (the prepared-state prewarm). Merge that one first; this one rebases cleanly onto main once it lands.

## Changes

- `src/agents/model-provider-auth.ts`:
  - `hasAuthForModelProvider` → `async` / `Promise<boolean>`.
  - `await new Promise<void>((r) => setImmediate(r))` inserted before the slow path.
  - `createProviderAuthChecker` returns an async closure.
  - New helper `resolveProviderAuthMap({ providers, ... })`: resolves a known provider set up-front via the async checker, returns a sync `(provider) => boolean` closure. Use when handing off to existing sync helpers.
  - Warmer's internal call awaits per-provider.
- `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.ts`: `resolveVisibleModelCatalog` is async; internal `.filter(...)` becomes a `for…of` with `await hasAuth(entry.provider)`.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.ts`: awaits `resolveVisibleModelCatalog`; the catalog auth-filter loop awaits per provider.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/models.ts`: awaits `resolveVisibleModelCatalog`.
- `src/flows/model-picker.ts`: three sites switched from `createProviderAuthChecker` to `resolveProviderAuthMap` over the relevant provider set — keeps the wizard's option-builder helpers sync.
- `src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts`: assertions migrated to `await expect(...).resolves.toBe(...)`.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts` — 5/5 passing
- [x] Local build (`./run.sh build`) clean (exit 0)
- [ ] Restart gateway with the branch; trigger `/models` from Discord during the warmer window (~50 s after startup) to exercise the slow path; confirm Discord ack does not time out and the liveness warning's `eventLoopDelayP99` drops well below the previous ~24 s
- [ ] Trigger `/models` again post-warm to confirm the fast path still returns sub-10 ms
target.author: sjf
